title: buchnera humifusa
intro: Buchnera humifusa, also known as Buchnera coriacea, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is primarily found in the Appalachian Mountains of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. Buchnera humifusa can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are said to offer health benefits such as relief from fever, sore throats, and skin irritations.
